front: Painting: title red-haired goddess stands on a conch shell surrounded by cupids and mermen; two centaurs blow conch shells
Painting: thirteen cherubs fly in the background; serpentine central figure lifted from the artist's previous painting The Nymphaeum
Painting: naked man knees another in the back and bites his throat while two poets watch
back: Dante and Virgil in Hell (William-Adolphe Bouguereau)

front: Painting: submitted for this painter's third attempt to win the Prix de Rome; depicts a scene from Inferno
back: Dante and Virgil in Hell (William-Adolphe Bouguereau)

front: Painting: four naked women drag a faun/satyr into a pool
Artist: Academic art was derisively called 'l'art pompier' because Attic helmets resembled firemen's headgear
Artist: won the Prix de Rome at 25 with Zenobia Found by Shepherds on the Banks of the Araxes
Artist: wife Elizabeth Jane Gardner said she'd 'rather be known as the best imitator of [this painter] than be nobody'
Artist: refused (with Cabanel) to let Impressionists into the 1863 Paris Salon, leading to the Salon des Refuses
Painting: Adam and Eve discovering the body of Abel; painted after the death of the artist's son
Painting: shawled barefoot girl on a concrete bench with a violin on her lap; Notre-Dame de Paris in background
Painting: John Berger's Ways of Seeing reproduces this painting of soaring nymphs to illustrate how men of state discussed under such paintings
Painting: fury depicted topless holding a torch, pointing at a stabbed woman
